info.title: Veteran Verification
info.description:
The Deprecated Veteran Verification API (v1) allows you to confirm the Veteran status
for a given person, or retrieve a Veteran's service history, disability rating,
or select flashes.

When confirming Veteran Status, the Status endpoint will return "Confirmed" or "Not Confirmed".

## Quickstart Guide

### 1. Get Access Credentials

Get started by filling out the form on the [Apply for VA Lighthouse Developer Access](/apply)
page.

After submitting a request, you will receive your credentials for using the API in the Sandbox
environment, which allows you to try it out with mock data before moving to the Production
environment.

### 2. Test the API

In the endpoint documentation below, we've provided a curl command builder for trying out the
API before implementation with your app.
Use the login credentials associated with each [Test User](https://github.com/department-of-veterans-affairs/vets-api-clients/blob/master/test_accounts.md)
you would like to work with.

### 3. Build your app

The base URI for the Veteran Verification API in the Sandbox environment is:

https://sandbox-api.va.gov/services/veteran_verification/v1

### 4. Show us a demo and get set up in the Production environment

After building your app, we ask that you give us a demo before we set you up with production
credentials. Please see the [Path to Production](/go-live) page for more details.

## Reference

### Sandbox vs. Production Data
APIs accessed via the Sandbox environment are using the same underlying logic as VA's production
APIs; only the underlying data store is different.

### Rate Limiting
We implemented basic rate limiting of 60 requests per minute. If you exceed this quota, your
request will return a 429 status code. You may petition for increased rate limits by emailing,
and requests will be decided on a case by case basis.

get.summary: Retrieve disability rating of authorized Veteran
get.description: This endpoint provides a veteran's combined and individual disability rating(s) and related decisions and dates.

get.summary: Retrieve service history of authorized Veteran
get.description:
This endpoint provides a veteran's detailed service history including all
military service episodes with the first and last name of the Veteran, the branch of
service, start and end dates, paygrade, discharge status, and separation reason.
It also provides start and end dates and locations for each deployment within each
episode of service.

get.summary: Confirms whether an individual is a Veteran
get.description: The Veteran Confirmation API allows you to confirm Veteran status for a given person. This can be useful for offering Veterans discounts or other benefits.

errors.title: Veteran not identifiable.
errors.detail: No data found for ID or demographics.

location.description: Three letter ISO country code of deployment location

Deployment.description: Deployment during a service history episode

branch_of_service.description: The branch of service such as National Guard, Coast Guard, Reserves, Army, Navy, Air Force, Marines

component_of_service.description: A component of service such as Reserves, National Guard

service_type.description: If veteran is on active duty or not

start_date.description: start date of a service history episode (YYYY-mm-dd)

end_date.description: end date of a service history episode (YYYY-mm-dd)

pay_grade.description:
Defines the level of compensation for a position, normalized across military branches.

Possible values include the concatenation of Pay Plan Code and Pay Grade Code.
Pay Plan Code is represented as a two-character code, and can be one of five values:
  - CC (Commissioned Corps)
  - MC (Cadet)
  - ME (Enlisted)
  - MO (Officer)
  - MW (Warrant Officer)

Pay Grade Code is a value between 01 and 10.

Pay Plan Code is concatenated with Pay Grade Code to determine the full Pay Grade value, with the leading character (M or C) stripped from Pay Plan Code.

discharge_status.description:
Character of discharge from service episode. Possible values are:

Both "honorable-for-va-purposes" and "dishonorable-for-va-purposes" represent a change in character of discharge based on an administrative decision, for purposes of VA benefits administration. The original character of discharge for other purposes was either "bad-conduct" or "other-than-honorable". "honorable-absence-of-negative-report" represents an unreported character of service that the Department of Defense classifies as honorable.

separation_reason.description: Additional text description for separation reason beyond discharge_status value

service_episode_combat_pay.description: If there is any combat pay in the VA Profile service's CombatPayBio with the same serviceEpisodeIdentifier, then indicate Y

active_duty_status.description: Indicates the Veteran's current active duty status

combat_service_indicator.description: Indicates whether the service member has ever been a part of active combat duty

duty_status_text.description: Relates to an individual's affiliation with the US Military.
duty_status_text.example: Active Component = full time active duty, not in the Guard or Reserve

total_number_of_regular_active_duty_days.description: Total number of regular active duty days

total_number_of_reserve_active_duty_days.description: Total number of reserve active duty days

total_number_of_guard_active_duty_days.description: Total number of guard active duty days

veteran_status.description: Whether the system could confirm the Veteran status of the authorized individual

VeteranStatusDetails.description: Veteran status confirmation for an individual
